{{Infobox madra
| name = Blue Light
| introduced = tg-03-city-of-light
}}

''See also: [[Blue Light Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue]] [[Light]] is a luminous force that can be summoned by [[Alin]]. When manifested, it appears as sapphire-colored tendrils or tentacles that erupt from the earth, sometimes reaching heights of up to ten feet and growing as thick as tree trunks. This power can serve various functions: it has been used to induce unconsciousness in individuals without killing them, and it can act as a protective barrier for [[Alin]] against external threats. The light also possesses a form of sentience, as it is capable of communicating through thought or speech to offer observations and instructions. Once its purpose is fulfilled, the [[essence]] of the [[Blue]] [[Light]] returns to [[Elysia]].}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue]] [[Light]] manifests as luminous, sapphire-like tendrils and tentacles that erupt from the ground with a thickness comparable to tree trunks. These translucent appendages possess the ability to bind targets and drain their strength, though they can also keep individuals unconscious without causing death. While the entity is a voice that has begged for mercy following the death of [[Helgard]], it also challenged [[Alin]] by claiming his desires were not rooted in justice. Now, [[Alin]] can summon a coil of the [[Blue]] [[Light]], which has challenged him to attempt a rescue.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] begged for mercy when [[Alin]] killed [[Helgard]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] manifests as translucent sapphire-like tendrils from the ground.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Blue Light]] consists of sapphire tendrils that can bind and drain strength.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Blue Light]] can be called out of the ground in tendrils.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] manifests as luminous blue tendrils and tentacles thick as tree trunks that erupt from the ground.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] can keep people unconscious without killing them.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] is a voice that begs for mercy.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] pointed out that [[Alin]]'s desire was not justice.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can summon a coil of [[Blue Light]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Blue Light]] tells [[Alin]] to save him if he can.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Madra]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
